root_cause.text: The Jackson2ExecutionContextStringSerializer enabled default typing without restrictions on the ObjectMapper, allowing arbitrary untrusted classes to be deserialized and leading to potential remote code execution.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces `this.objectMapper.enableDefaultTyping();` with a custom trusted default typing mechanism (`this.objectMapper.setDefaultTyping(createTrustedDefaultTyping());`) that validates incoming class types against an allowlist of trusted classes (`TrustedTypeIdResolver`).

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The Jackson2ExecutionContextStringSerializer enabled default typing without restrictions on the ObjectMapper, allowing arbitrary untrusted classes to be deserialized and leading to potential remote code execution.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch replaces `this.objectMapper.enableDefaultTyping();` with a custom trusted default typing mechanism (`this.objectMapper.setDefaultTyping(createTrustedDefaultTyping());`) that validates incoming class types against an allowlist of trusted classes (`TrustedTypeIdResolver`).

intent_record.intent_summary: Update copyright year in the header comment.
intent_record.what: Updated the end year in the copyright notice from 2019 to 2020.
intent_record.how: This change has no impact on program execution or behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Document and import necessary Jackson classes to support secure polymorphic deserialization.
intent_record.what: Added imports for Jackson polymorphic type validation and updated Javadoc to document secure deserialization practices.
intent_record.how: Provides developers with guidance and the necessary classes to configure safe default typing and explicit mappings for trusted classes in the execution context.

intent_record.intent_summary: Secure the Jackson ObjectMapper against unsafe polymorphic deserialization vulnerabilities.
intent_record.what: Replaced the default ObjectMapper typing with a configuration that blocks unsafe polymorphic base types and uses a custom trusted default typing method.
intent_record.how: Restricts the classes that can be deserialized from JSON, mitigating potential remote code execution vulnerabilities associated with Jackson's default polymorphic deserialization.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a trusted type resolver to prevent insecure deserialization vulnerabilities in Jackson.
intent_record.what: Added a set of classes (TrustedTypeResolverBuilder, TrustedTypeIdResolver) and methods to enforce a whitelist of trusted classes during Jackson JSON deserialization.
intent_record.how: Interceps the type resolution process in Jackson and throws an IllegalArgumentException if the class being deserialized is not explicitly trusted via a predefined list, mixin, or Jackson annotation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update copyright year in the license header to 2020.
intent_record.what: Updated the copyright year in the header comment from 2018 to 2020.
intent_record.how: This change has no effect on runtime behavior or program execution.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add JsonTypeInfo import to support JSON-related testing.
intent_record.what: Adds an import statement for the Jackson JsonTypeInfo annotation.
intent_record.how: Makes the JsonTypeInfo class available in the test file context, likely for JSON serialization/deserialization setup.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix JSON deserialization by including type information in the serialized output.
intent_record.what: Added the @JsonTypeInfo annotation to the ComplexObject class.
intent_record.how: Ensures that fully qualified class name information is included during JSON serialization and deserialization.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update copyright year to 2020 in the license header.
intent_record.what: Updated the copyright year range in the license header.
intent_record.how: This change has no functional impact on the code execution, as it only modifies a comment.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add necessary imports and author tag to prepare for new test cases in Jackson2ExecutionContextStringSerializerTests.
intent_record.what: Added Java I/O, Util, Jackson, and JUnit imports, along with a new author tag to the test class.
intent_record.how: Provides the necessary class dependencies and test annotations required to implement new serialization test cases.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add tests for mapped and unmapped type deserialization in Jackson2ExecutionContextStringSerializer
intent_record.what: Added new test methods and nested classes to verify serialization behavior.
intent_record.how: Validates that the serializer handles mapped types with type info properly and rejects unmapped types during deserialization.

grouping_result.intent: Update copyright year to 2020 in the license header.
Add a trusted type resolver to prevent insecure deserialization vulnerabilities in Jackson.
Add tests for mapped and unmapped type deserialization in Jackson2ExecutionContextStringSerializer
Fix JSON deserialization by including type information in the serialized output.

consistency_review.core_intent: Update copyright year to 2020 in the license header.
Secure Jackson ObjectMapper against insecure polymorphic deserialization vulnerabilities.
Add tests and related imports for mapped and unmapped type deserialization in Jackson2ExecutionContextStringSerializer.

relevance_review.reason: The changes only update the copyright year in the license header, which is completely unrelated to the Jackson deserialization vulnerability.

The group directly addresses the vulnerability by securing the Jackson ObjectMapper against unsafe polymorphic deserialization and implementing a trusted type resolver, which mitigates the unrestricted default typing issue.
The group adds tests for mapped and unmapped type deserialization in the vulnerable serializer, which directly verifies the fix for the unrestricted default typing vulnerability.
The group modifies how type information is included during JSON serialization/deserialization, directly addressing the core issue of how default typing was handled in the ObjectMapper.

candidates.rationale: This line enables default typing globally on the ObjectMapper without any restrictions, leading to insecure deserialization.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement globally enables default typing on the ObjectMapper without any restrictions, which allows arbitrary untrusted classes to be deserialized, leading to the remote code execution vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
This commit adds a ExecutionContextSerializer based on Jackson2.

It has been tested with Jackson 2.3.3 which is the Jac
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 22109eea84e4ea5ab773f58baad2b366b1858591
Author: Marten Deinum <mdeinum@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Mar 7 09:19:57 2016 +0100

    This commit adds a ExecutionContextSerializer based on Jackson2.
    
    It has been tested with Jackson 2.3.3 which is the Jackson version
    used with the Spring dependency.
    
    Tests have been updated to use Hamcrest matchers and made a bit more reusable.

diff --git a/spring-batch-core/src/main/java/org/springframework/batch/core/repository/dao/Jackson2E
bic_trace_step.reason: The constructor Jackson2ExecutionContextStringSerializer() explicitly calls `this.objectMapper.enableDefaultTyping();`, which enables default typing without any restrictions, matching the root cause of the vulnerability. There is no fix present in this commit to restrict the allowed types.
